text: I arrive as junior developer learning where to start. My past: I know Git basics but product menus and enterprise language can make me feel like I do not belong yet.
Today I want to: Find a clear beginner path without drowning in product categories.
My life goal is: Become employable without losing confidence to confusing tooling.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub

text: I see "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub".
I think: I see GitHub's big promise, but I do not yet see the beginner doorway I need.
My reaction: My first reaction is cautious interest mixed with intimidation; the headline sounds confident but broad.
Idea: show a clear "Start learning GitHub" path for beginners before the product menu gets dense.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month to explore is worth it if the next screen helps me find a beginner path toward employability.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that GitHub feels important but not yet personally welcoming from this screen. Confidence: medium.
